En: They're *good* apes, actually.
Zh: “实际上，他们是*好*猩猩。”

En: I don't know about that... She turns east, to the bombed out buildings lining the waterfront, then shivers slightly.
Zh: “这我就不知道了……”她转向东边，面向海滨炸毁的建筑，稍稍打了个寒颤。

En: I don't know about that... She turns north, to the bombed out buildings lining the waterfront, then shivers slightly.
Zh: “这我就不知道了……”她转向北边，面向海滨炸毁的建筑，稍稍打了个寒颤。

En: Did the communists and the anarchists shoot back?
Zh: “康米主义者和无政府主义者反击了吗？”

En: You could say they got *totally* shot in the head.
Zh: “你可以说他们的脑袋*全都*挨了枪子。”

En: Indeed. They piled them in mass graves in Ozonne and, well -- that's the last anyone heard of those people.
Zh: “确实。他们把尸体堆在欧佐纳尔的大坟场，在那之后——就再也没有人听到他们的消息。”

En: She likes the *totality* of it.
Zh: 她喜欢这个*整体*。

En: Did they ever. Before they got shot themselves, they shot two million people.
Zh: “他们怎么没有。在自己中枪之前，他们射杀了200万人。”

En: Who got the mineral rights?
Zh: “那矿产权落在谁手里了？”

En: The liberals got the mineral rights. She looks up to the sky, then inland at the crumbling village. The wind blows. "And by mineral rights, I mean *everything*." \n
Zh: “自由派拿到了矿产权。”她抬头看向天空，然后望向摇摇欲坠的城市。风在呼啸。“说到矿产权，我的意思其实是指*一切*。”

En: The liberals got the mineral rights. She looks up to the sky, then inland at the crumbling city. "And by mineral rights, I mean *everything*."
Zh: “自由派拿到了矿产权。”她抬头看向天空，然后望向摇摇欲坠的城市。“说到矿产权，我的意思其实是指*一切*。”

En: Okay, and by *liberals* you mean...
Zh: “好吧，那你口中的*自由派*是指……”

En: Liberals are usually middle-class people, detective. Or the remaining gentry. The beneficiaries of the pre-revolutionary *arrangement*.
Zh: “自由派一般都是中产阶级，警探。或者是剩下来的上流阶层。大革命*筹划*之前的受益者。”

En: Some were rich enough to stay with the Constitution; with monarchy -- *big mistake*. Others bet on the Revolution, they were called the Ultras, or ultraliberals. They fared well.
Zh: “有些人有钱到能与宪法与君主制并驾齐驱——这是个*大错误*。其他人押注在大革命上，他们被称为极端分子，或者是极端自由主义者。他们过得很好。”

En: How did the liberals win it all?
Zh: “自由派是怎么赢得这一切的？”

En: They didn't *win* so much as survive. *We* were the last ones standing when the war ended -- everyone else got shot in the head, remember?
Zh: “与其说是*赢了*，不如说他们活了下来。当战争结束时，*我们*是最后活下来的那些——其他人都被射杀了，还记得吗？”

En: If everyone got shot, who was there to surrender to?
Zh: “如果所有人都被射杀了，那是要向谁投降呢？”

En: To foreign intervention -- the Coalition. Those people *really* took the mineral rights.
Zh: “外国干涉势力——联盟。那些人*真正地*拿走了矿产权。”

En: Wait. You just said the liberals already took everything.
Zh: “等等。你刚才不是说自由派已经夺走一切了吗。”

En: The liberals took everything that wasn't nailed to the ground -- the Coalition took the *ground*, she stomps her rubber booted foot. "The ocean, the laws and the people."
Zh: “自由派夺走了没有钉在土地上的一切——但是联盟夺走了*土地*。”她跺了跺橡胶雨鞋。“海洋，法律，还有人民。”

En: Who are the Coalition?
Zh: “联盟是谁？”

En: The Coalition of Nations. Graad, Mesque, Vesper, Messina, Oranje and Sur-La-Clef -- the armed centre of the world. They landed here and ended the Revolution. It was the *moralist* thing to do.
Zh: “联盟国。格拉德、梅斯克、维斯珀、梅西纳、奥兰治还有苏拉菲——整个世界的军备中心。这是一件*道德家*要做的事。”

En: There is bitterness in her voice -- tempered with understanding. She is critical, but ultimately understands the cause.
Zh: 她的声音里隐含着一股苦涩——被理解缓和了一下。她是个批判主义者，但最后还是能理解个中缘由。

En: Moralist?
Zh: “道德家？”

En: The moralists believe in keeping everything exactly the way it is. They believe in mineral rights -- and not shooting people in the head...
Zh: “道德家相信要让一切保持应有的样子。他们相信矿产权——也相信不要朝人类的脑袋开枪……”

En: At least not in the same *manner and volume* as the others do. They are the long-standing provisional rulers of Revachol now -- the Coalition Government.
Zh: “至少不会和其他人的*方式和数量*一样。现在，他们——也就是联盟政府，是瑞瓦肖长期的临时统治者。”

En: This is their Zone of Control. They embolden the RCM with crumbs of the same law they took. Technically speaking -- *you* are a moralist.
Zh: “这里就是他们的控制区。他们鼓励RCM施行与其相同的法律。从技术的角度来讲——*你*就是一个道德家。”

En: The colour of moralism is blue. The official motto of the Moralintern, or Moralist International, is: 'A blue forget-me-not; a piece of the grey sky'. Unofficial: 'For a moment, there was hope'.
Zh: 道德主义是蓝色的。国道，或者说国际道德组织的官方箴言是：*一束蓝色勿忘我；一片灰色的天空*。非官方版本是：*有那么一瞬间，我们感觉到希望*。

En: Not just technically -- practically. For a moment, there was hope.
Zh: “不仅仅是技术角度——实际上就是。有那么一瞬间，希望是存在的。”

En: Not just technically -- practically as well. I like what I've heard about these people.
Zh: “不仅仅是技术角度——实际上也是这样。就我听到的信息来说，我喜欢这群人。”

En: I don't think I am moralist, ma'am.
Zh: “我不觉得自己是个道德家，夫人。”

En: If always picking the option that doesn't commit to anything then hell yes I am -- and also not.
Zh: “如果总是选择而不用承诺的话，那我还真他妈就是——但也不是。”

En: An almost endearingly moralist standpoint, she bows. "It must be hard to take a moderate approach to head-shooting in your line of work."
Zh: “差不多算是个讨巧的道德主义观点，”她弯下腰。“在你的工作领域，要想换种温和的方式来朝人脑门子开枪，肯定很难吧。”

En: Of course, she sizes you up. "Not easy to be moderate about *head-shooting* in your line of work."
Zh: “当然，”她打量了你一番。“在你的工作领域，要想温和地*朝人脑门子开枪*应该不容易。”

En: A devout man of the centre, she nods. "Hard to come by. It's good to have someone who takes a moderate approach to head-shooting -- in your line of work I mean."
Zh: “一个虔诚的中间派人士，”她点了点头。“很难见到。我是想说在你的工作领域，还能有人用一种温和的方式朝人脑门子开枪，其实挺好的。”

En: Rooty-tooty pointy shooty!
Zh: 枪！

En: (Cock your finger pistols.) "Rooty tooty pointy shooty!"
Zh: （举起手指做出持枪的手势）“枪！”

En: Rooty tooty pointy shooty!
That's the way of History. She nods.
Zh: “这就是历史。”她点点头。

En: Or -- you could *not* eat all the candy at once, let the ma'am rest for a while, and return to this later?
Zh: “或者——你可以*不要*这么囫囵吞枣，让这位夫人稍微休息一下，之后再来？”

En: Later, yes. Let my mind cool a bit.
Zh: “等一会儿，可以。让我的大脑先冷静一下。”

En: No. More. NOW.
Zh: “不。我要我知道更多。现在就要。”

En: Voracious, she nods approvingly. "I'm up for it. What'll it be?"
Zh: “你很贪婪，”她赞许地点点头。“我已经准备好了。还想知道什么？”

En: Too much *reality* at once can prove too much, she nods. "There's plenty of time later. For now, is there anything *else* I can help you with?"
Zh: “一次接受太多*现实*可能让人吃不消，”她点点头。“之后还会有大把的时间。那么，还有什么*别的事*可以帮你吗？”

En: I don't care about kings. Tell me one more thing...
Zh: “我不关心国王的事。再给我说说……”

En: About head-shooting? She smiles.
Zh: “脑袋挨枪子的事吗？”她微笑着。

En: The rulers of Revachol -- and also *the world*. These guys are strong.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖——还有*这个世界*的统治者。这些家伙很强大。

En: Indeed. Tzaraath is a highly infectious microorganism that destroyed brain tissue. The actual causes of the Revolution were material. The pandemic only provided the spark.
Zh: “没错。霉斑是一种传染性极高的细菌性疾病，可以摧毁人类的大脑组织。大革命真正的起因是物质。瘟疫不过是导火索而已。”

En: Welcome to reality, baby.
Zh: “欢迎来到现实世界，亲爱的。”

En: No. The tiny apes are doing all they can to be better. It's not their fault.
Zh: 不是。这些小猩猩在尽一切可能变得更好。这不是他们的错。

En: The apes will never change, they are all evil. End of discussion.
Zh: 猩猩是永远也不会变的，它们都是邪恶的。这个话题的讨论到此为止。

En: It takes time for the apes to change. And work. Perhaps what happened here was part of that time -- and part of that work.
Zh: 猩猩改变是需要时间的。还有努力。也许那个时候，这里发生的一切就是时间的一部分——还有努力。

En: I *loved* that king. Fucking communists...
Zh: “我*喜欢*那个国王。那帮该死的康米主义者……”

En: Which one? Actually -- it doesn't matter. King Guillaume has always been my favourite. He could smell a PR disaster brewing. So he got out alive and his nephew Frissel got shot in his place...
Zh: “哪个？其实——不重要了。我一直最喜欢纪尧姆国王。他能够感觉到一场公关灾难正在酝酿。所以他能活下来，但是坐了他位置的侄子——弗里塞尔就被枪杀了……”

En: We -- she's one of them. Of course.
Zh: 我们——她也是其中之一。这是理所当然。

En: Then *you* happened. Your youth in the Thirties.
Zh: 然后*你*出现了。你的青春属于30年代。

En: She is not at all fazed by your genocidal sabre rattling.
Zh: 她完全没有因为你那滔滔不绝的种族灭绝言论而焦虑。

En: A counterargument, but still -- your historic mass-murderer fantasies have only endeared you to this person.
Zh: 一场辩论，不过——你那历史性的大屠杀幻想让这个人对你的好感度变高了。

En: Would you say it was a bunch of apes dukin' it out?
Zh: “你会说是一群猩猩在决一死战吗？”

En: Why of course -- we're talking duke-out central. Full-swing intraspecies warfare.
Zh: “怎么了，当然是啊——我们说的就是决一死战。全面展开的种内战争。”

En: Now's your chance -- ask her who she is -- she won't get out this time.
Zh: 你的机会来了——询问她是谁——这次她肯定逃不掉了。

En: What is the Zone of Control?
Zh: “控制区是什么？”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: And the apes -- were they evil?
Zh: “那些猩猩——很邪恶吗？”

En: No. I would say the apes were *neutral*. She looks at you, her gaze sharp.
Zh: “没有。要我说的话，那群猩猩算*中立*吧。”她看向你，目光锐利。

En: The Zone of Control is the third incarnation of Revachol. After the failure of the Suzerain and the Commune.
Zh: “控制区是瑞瓦肖的第三个化身，就在宗主国和公社失败之后。”

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The charge dissipates into the dark water. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。控诉消散在黑水中。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: Sounds like evil to me.
Zh: “在我看来挺邪恶的。”

En: Okay -- neutral.
Zh: “好吧——中立。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building.
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。

En: Then you would have died fighting against them. Long before the Beachhead... our time is a shadow cast by that day.
Zh: “如果跟他们动手的话，你早就应该死了。早在抢滩登陆之前……我们现在还生活在那天投下的阴影之下。”

En: On the other hand... She turns east, to the bombed out buildings lining the waterfront. "Maybe you're right."
Zh: “话又说回来……”她转向东边，面向海滨炸毁的建筑。“也许你是对的。”

En: On the other hand... She turns north, to the bombed out buildings lining the waterfront. "Maybe you're right."
Zh: “话又说回来……”她转向北边，面向海滨炸毁的建筑。“也许你是对的。”

En: On the other hand... She turns east, to the bombed out buildings lining the waterfront, then shivers slightly.
Zh: “话又说回来……”她转向东边，面向海滨炸毁的建筑，身体微微颤抖。

En: On the other hand... She turns north, to the bombed out buildings lining the waterfront, then shivers slightly.
Zh: “话又说回来……”她转向北边，面向海滨炸毁的建筑，身体微微颤抖。

En: The wind stops, there is silence on the dark water of the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，马丁内斯水湾黑暗的水面上，一片寂静……

En: It was a kerfuffle all right, the lieutenant mumbles from behind his notes.
Zh: “确实是一团混乱，”警监埋头记录，喃喃自语。

En: No way. I wouldn't have fought with the communards.
Zh: “不可能。我是不会跟公社战斗的。”

En: Yes, an acute thiamine deficiency can be exacerbated by alcoholism. She blinks her green eyes. "Exacerbated means 'made worse.'"
Zh: “是啊，酗酒会让硫胺素缺乏的症状恶化。”她眨了眨绿色的眼睛。“恶化就意味着变得更糟糕。”

En: I see, yes. A deluge of geopolitical questions.
Zh: “我明白了，是的。像洪水一样泛滥的地缘问题。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: I had a deluge too. In my head. (Point to your little head.)
Zh: “我也有同样泛滥的问题，在我的脑子里。”（指向你的小脑袋。）

En: Truly a *kerfuffle*.
Zh: “真是一团*混乱*。”

En: Him and tens of thousands of his *wonderfully* fascist kingsmen. It was a *wild* time.
Zh: “他，还有他那群成千上万*优秀的*法西斯主义军人。那可真是个*疯狂*的时代。”

En: IT'S ALL YOURS.
Zh: 全都是你的了。

En: POCKMARKED BY CANNONS.
Zh: 大炮留下的凹痕。

En: Yes, King Guillaume had a nose for bad PR. He ran before it -- what is the expression -- *went down*? Anyway, Gil got out alive and his nephew Frissel got shot in his place...
Zh: “没错，纪尧姆国王对糟糕的公共关系很敏锐。”在那之前他就跑了——那句话怎么说来着——*退位*？不管怎么说，纪尧姆活着逃了出来，他的侄子弗里塞尔坐了他的位置，然后死了……”

En: I prefer the term *risk averse*. King Guillaume was nobody's fool -- he could smell a PR disaster brewing. So he got out alive and his nephew Frissel got shot in his place...
Zh: “我更喜欢*风险规避*这个词。纪尧姆国王可不是个傻瓜——他能够感觉到一场公关灾难正在酝酿。所以他能活下来，但是坐了他位置的侄子——弗里塞尔就被枪杀了……”

En: Smart king.
Zh: “聪明的国王。”

En: Cowardly king.
Zh: “懦弱的国王。”

En: THERE IS PEACE IN THE HEART.
Zh: 心里得到了平静。

En: With privatized healthcare and a work ethic that does not turn your brain into sponge tissue.
Zh: “有了私有化的医疗保健和职业道德，你的脑袋就不会变成海绵组织。”

En: The real king abdicated and lived out a long and productive life as a venture capitalist in Graad.
Zh: “真国王退位了，在格拉德做了一个风险资本家，度过了漫长而又充实的一生。”

En: Oh, lots of people. Even the king got shot in the head, or thrown beneath a horse. Or drowned. Accounts differ. It was  unceremonious. She shakes her head. "Just as well -- he wasn't actually the king. Just the king's nephew."
Zh: “哦，很多人啊。就连国王的脑袋都挨了一颗枪子，要么就是被扔到了马蹄下，或者是淹死了。说法都不一样。实在是太粗野了。”她摇了摇头。“不过也无妨——他也不是真正的国王。只不过是国王的侄子。”

En: Anyone else get shot in the head -- on the opposing side?
Zh: “还有谁的脑袋挨枪子了——我是说对面的？”

En: Until it's all mine -- every pigment-drenched black leaf of money in Revachol.
Zh: “直到一切都变成我的——瑞瓦肖每一分每一毫的黑钱。”

En: THE AGE OF MAN IS OVER.
Zh: 人类的时代已经结束了。

En: IT'S ALL YOURS.
Zh: 全都是你的。

En: CAPITAL IS PAST.
Zh: 资本家都是昨日黄花。

En: And after that?
Zh: “在那之后呢？”

En: The Twenties saw a decade of urban war: West of the river levelled, offshore platforms in flames... Still, it's regarded as an improvement on what came before: '08 to '19 was simply hell...
Zh: “二十年代经历了十年的城市战争：河流西边被夷为平地，海上平台化为灰烬……不过相比之前发生的事，已经能看作是一种进步了：08年到19年那会儿，基本上就是地狱……”

En: REVACHOL FOREVER.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖万岁。

En: The Thirties? Things settled down in the Thirties. Revachol East transformed itself into the world's largest tax haven -- with the international community's blessing. For the first time in a long time it seemed like things were *going* somewhere.
Zh: “30年代？那会儿情况稳定很多了。瑞瓦肖东部变成了世界上最大的避税场所——这一切都多亏了国际社会的保佑。长久以来，事情好像第一次取得了*进展*。”

En: This does not represent the official RCM opinion, the lieutenant says from behind his notes.
Zh: “这不代表RCM官方的意见，”警督还是埋头记着。

En: Don't worry, Kras Mazov shot fifteen million people in the head. But that was all the way over in Graad.
Zh: “别担心，卡拉斯·马佐夫杀了1500万人。不过那都是在格拉德的事了。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: A quietly simmering rage.
Zh: 怒气在一片寂静中即将爆发。

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: Yes. The *Insulindian Deluge* they call it.
Zh: “是啊。大家都叫它*伊苏林迪大洪水*。”

En: All that untaxed income must have fuelled *The New*. That can only mean one thing...
Zh: 那些免税收入一定是刺激了*新时代*。这只意味着一件事情……

En: Yes -- the red deluge. The *Insulindian Deluge* they call it.
Zh: “是的——红色赤潮。大家都叫它*伊苏林迪大洪水*。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: No. It was a market mirage fuelled by cocaine and quantitative easing. The Forties *dispelled* it like a cold splash. An isola-wide hangover, you might say. And here we are... She curtsies.
Zh: “不对。那是在可卡因和量化宽松刺激下，为市场造了个海市蜃楼。四十年代又把它当做一瓢冷水全部泼了出去，*消除*的干干净净。你可以说，那是一场全洲范围内的宿醉。然后就到了现在……”她行了个屈膝礼。

En: That's when they discovered Disco.
Zh: “那是他们发明迪斯科的年代。”

En: Were they?
Zh: “哪里有进展？”

En: Yes. And quantitative easing. It was a market mirage, unfortunately. The Forties dispelled it. An isola-wide hangover, you might say. So, here we are... She curtsies.
Zh: “是啊。还有量化宽松。不过很可惜，那不过市场的海市蜃楼。到了四十年代就消除殆尽了。你可以说，那是一场全洲范围内的宿醉。然后就到了现在……”她行了个屈膝礼。

En: A dog barks and a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: Until the middle class, the gentry, and the workers are all gone and the age of man is over.
Zh: “直到中产阶级、上流社会人士、还有工人全部消失，人类的时代也结束了。”

En: Welcome to reality, baby.
Zh: “欢迎来到现实世界，亲爱的。”

En: That's a tragedy.
Zh: “真是个悲剧。”

En: Khm. The lieutenant puts down his notes for a moment. "Opinions expressed here do not reflect the official position of the RCM."
Zh: “咳咳。”警督稍微放下了手中的笔记。“这里发表的意见和言论不代表RCM的官方立场。”

En: Sounds like they should have shot more people in the head then.
Zh: “听上去他们那会儿应该处决了不少人。”

En: All she can see are vague outlines of a great winding down. The doldrums. People killed, but no great transitions of wealth. It makes her feel old -- and something else. Something you can't put your finger on...
Zh: 她能看见的只有大宽松那模糊不清的轮廓。景气停滞。人们被杀，但是没有发生巨大的财富转移。这让她感觉到苍老——还有其他一些东西。某些你也说不清楚的东西……

En: For her to be where she is Wild Pines Group must have picked the right side.
Zh: 对于她来说，她所处的野松公司一定是选择了正确的那一边。

En: Something tells you her life and yours are not that similar. Maybe it's because she has a boat and you have... that necktie? A pair of pants?
Zh: 有些东西告诉你，你和她的生活不太一样。也许是因为她拥有一艘船，而你拥有……一条领带？还是一条裤子？

En: Coupris is still around, aren't they?
Zh: “库普瑞斯还在，对吗？”

En: Forever? She cocks her head sideways. "In twenty years Revachol went from a king to a commune to a Zone of Control. She *is* forever. But the next thing she will *be* is something else."
Zh: “永远？”她把头歪向一边。“20年来，瑞瓦肖从一个国家变成了一个公社，后来又变成了控制区。她*的确*是永恒的。但是接下来，她会*变成*其他不同的东西。”

En: From the looks of it the Fifties haven't been much better for the Zone of Control -- you can see it in her eyes. Days slipping away....
Zh: 从表面看来，50年代成为控制区也没有好到哪里去——你可以从她的眼睛里看出来。日子就这样一天天溜走……

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The charge dissipates into the dark water. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。控诉消散在黑水中。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: She's silent a moment, contemplating. When she finally speaks, her tone is wistful: "We could have had so much *more*. Every one of us -- if only we'd played it right."
Zh: 她注视着你，沉默了片刻。当她终于开口时，语气中充满了渴望：“我们本来可以拥有*更多*的。每一个人——要是我们做对了的话。”

En: Past? She cocks her head to one side. "That's what Dobreva, the leader of the communards said, before she got a bullet in her head. Don't get me wrong..." There's sadness in her voice...
Zh: “结束？”她把头歪向一旁。“那是公社领袖杜博阿夫说的，后来她的脑袋也挨了枪子。别误会我的意思……”她的声音里隐隐透露出悲伤……

En: Her voice is almost wistful. "We could have had so much *more*. Every one of us -- if only we'd played it right."
Zh: 她的声音几乎可以说是充满着渴望。“我们本来可以拥有*更多*的。每一个人——要是我们做对了的话。”

En: All yours? She nods. "That's been the motto of many an indotribe. There is something *clarifying* about that strain of ultraliberalism..."
Zh: “都是你的？”她点点头。“很多寡头都把这句话当成座右铭。对于这种极端自由主义，有些地方需要*澄清*……”

En: Over? She nods. "That's *entropolism*, the very height of late Modernity: evacuate this world. You may prove a truly Modern Man yet."
Zh: “结束？”她点点头。“那还挺有*灰域迁移运动*风格的，晚期现代性的高度：从这个世界撤离。也许你确实是个真正的现代人。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Liquidate the rabid, prion-infested *dogs* who want to kill everyone -- with cleansing artillery fire.
Zh: “清算那些感染了朊病毒的狂暴*疯狗*，它们想杀掉所有人——用所谓的炮火的洗礼。”

En: You don't seem to be thrilled...
Zh: “你好像一点也不激动……”

En: I've no right to be dissatisfied, she shakes her head. "This shirt is Barbara Muskova. This raincoat is impervious to rain and is guaranteed for a hundred years, my daughters will wear it..." She looks at the eternite roofs and cinder blocks soaking in the sea air.
Zh: “我没有不满意的权利，”她摇了摇头。“这件衬衫是芭芭拉・穆斯科娃的。这件雨衣可以完全防雨，而且拥有100年的质保期，我的女儿都能穿……”她看着那片亘石屋顶，还有那浸透在海风中的灰色煤渣砖。

En: I've no right to be dissatisfied, she shakes her head. "This shirt is Barbara Muskova. This raincoat is impervious to rain and is guaranteed for a hundred years, my daughters will wear it. No, it's just..." She looks at the crumbling tenements, paint flecking from the stone...
Zh: “我没有不满意的权利，”她摇了摇头。“这件衬衫是芭芭拉·穆斯科娃的。这件雨衣可以完全防雨，而且拥有100年的质保期，我的女儿都能穿。不对，它只是……”她看着那栋摇摇欲坠、油漆斑驳的公寓大楼……

En: Good. Not that it would have mattered -- in the end, the Commune forced everyone to the barricades.
Zh: “很好。倒不是说这很重要——最后公社把所有人都逼到了屏障后面。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building.
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。

En: The wind stops, there is silence on the dark water of the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，马丁内斯水湾黑暗的水面上，一片寂静……

En: Happily.
Zh: “我很乐意。”

En: Believe it or not, Wild Pines isn't even the most notorious example -- that would be Saint-Baptiste. You can go quite far with that attitude.
Zh: “不管你信不信，野松公司甚至都不是最声名狼藉的范例——而应该是圣巴蒂斯特。有你这种态度，应该能走得很远。”

En: The cold runs down your spine -- she gestures toward the waters.
Zh: 一股寒意顺着你的脊椎一路向下窜——她的手指向水面。

En: That's enough about the times.
Zh: “时代的问题已经说的差不多了。”

En: Oh, and the anarchists too! They shot them well. So well one forgets they even existed.
Zh: “哦，还有无政府主义者！他们的枪法也挺好的。好到大家都快忘记他们的存在。”

En: Then you would have died, most likely. Not far from here -- maybe even *right* here, during the Beachhead, defending the coast the day the Coalition took the city.
Zh: “那样的话，你怕是早就已经死了。可能离这儿不远——也许*就在这里*，就在抢滩登陆的那一天，那天你在守卫海岸，而联盟夺走了这座城市。”

En: Which side was Wild Pines Group on?
Zh: “野松公司站在哪一边？”

En: She's not gloating. It's a relieved celebration.
Zh: 她没有幸灾乐祸。这是一场宽慰的庆典。

En: Anxious to hear more about mineral rights or who got shot in the head? A flash of teeth in her feisty grin.
Zh: “还想听我讲更多矿产权的事？还是谁谁谁的脑袋挨枪子的事？”她咧开嘴笑了，牙齿闪闪发光。

En: So a quarter of humanity... she calculates, "...simply lost their minds? And how would you stop a prion -- a complex folding protein; *un-life* -- with the technology 50 years ago?"
Zh: “所以，四分之一的人类……”她计算着，“……就这么简单的失去了理智？而且如何阻止朊病毒——那是一种复杂的折叠蛋白质；*非生命体*——而且使用的还是50年前的技术？”

En: That's it then. Another topic.
Zh: “那就先说到这里吧。换个话题。”

En: Those would be the communists. Generally speaking, 40 million people got shot in the head during the World Revolution. But the communists -- they *all* got shot in the head.
Zh: “康米主义者。总的来说，在世界革命期间，有4000万人被处决。但是康米主义者——他们*全部*都被杀了。”

En: Modernity. They developed the marvels of interisolary communication, telematic milieus, radiation, coloured plastics. Meanwhile, in Revachol West, the *aftermath* continues for the fifth decade.
Zh: “现代化。他们创造了各洲间交互通讯、信息通讯介质、辐射，还有彩色塑料的奇迹。与此同时，在瑞瓦肖西部，*余波*持续了整整50年。”

En: What have we been doing all that time?
Zh: “那段时间我们都做了些什么？”

En: Capital *is* finished. But understand this -- its end won't *free* anyone. It will only lead to more suffering.
Zh: “首都*已经*毁掉了。但是要理解一点——它的结束不会*解放*任何人。只会带来更多苦难。”

En: Here's some *wisdom* lady. SAY THE DEATH THING.
Zh: 这儿有一位*充满智慧*的女士。口里却诉说着死亡。

En: Our lives are very different from each other.
Zh: “我们俩的生活有很大的不同。”

En: Our lives are not the same.
Zh: “我们的生活不一样。”

En: No doubt -- but we share the same time and position on the planet's crust. That counts for more than you'd think.
Zh: “确实如此——不过我们在这颗星球的地壳上，分享着相同的时间和位置。这一点比你想象的更重要。”

En: Who got shot in the head?
Zh: “谁被杀了？”

En: Of course not. It was a highly infectious microorganism that destroyed brain tissue. The actual causes of the Revolution were material. The pandemic only provided the spark.
Zh: “当然不是。它是一种拥有高度传染性的微生物，可以摧毁大脑组织。革命真正的起因是物质。瘟疫不过是导火索而已。”

En: Wow, really?
Zh: “哇哦，真的吗？”

En: I don't know what I would have done differently.
Zh: “我不知道自己会不会做出不同的选择。”

En: A cold creeps up your spine, reaching its tendrils up your neck, toward the back of your head...
Zh: 一股寒意顺着你的脊椎向上窜，爬上你的颈项，朝后脑勺一路延伸过去……

En: I would have sought a medical solution. Sounds like *tzaraath* drove those people mad.
Zh: “我会寻求医学手段来解决问题。听起来像是*霉斑*把那些人都逼疯了。”

En: Your jaw clenches -- give the lady a taste of your *wisdom*.
Zh: 你咬紧下颌——让那位女士品味一下你的*智慧*。

En: My position, ma'am? My parents got ripped to shreds in the Revolution -- I would have gone the same way. I was saved by being two years old. That's my position -- the abattoir.
Zh: “我的立场，夫人？我的父母在革命中被撕成了碎片——我本来也会遭受相同的命运。我能获救是因为当时我只有两岁。这就是我的立场——那是一个屠宰场。”

En: Okay. Maybe it's impossible. What happened had to happen. And always will.
Zh: “好吧。也许是不可能。该发生的总会发生，而且注定会发生。”

En: That's not Coupris, dear. It's just a brand name, snatched up by a Vespertine holding company. No, the real Coupris is as dead as the king they bet on.
Zh: “那不是库普瑞斯，亲爱的。那只是薄暮控股公司旗下一个品牌的名字。不，真正的库普瑞斯就跟他们押注的国王一样，早就死了。”

En: Good hygiene, really? A very *moderate* solution to an *extreme* problem. It's those sort of half-measures that doomed the authorities in Graad...
Zh: “良好的个人卫生习惯，你说真的吗？对于一个*极端*问题来说，确实是个很*温和的*解决办法。正是这种折中的办法，让格拉德当局陷入了绝境……”

En: Liquidate? She nods. "That's what King Filippe kept screaming from the Sol Aurum. *Liquidate, liquidate!*" she says in a sing-song-y kind of way.
Zh: “清算？”她点点头。“菲利普国王一直在索尔·奥利姆嚷嚷着这些。*清算，清算！*”她用一种抑扬顿挫的腔调说到。

En: Not so fast -- who is she in all this? Ask her who she is, she owes you an answer.
Zh: 别这么着急——她又是谁？问问她是谁，她欠你一个答案。

En: What did this tzaraath do?
Zh: “这个霉斑是怎么回事？”

En: It made people overthrow their governments.
Zh: “它会让人们推翻他们的政府。”

En: It was a funny time in history. They'd discovered transistors and rock music, but they didn't know anything about prions. Nobody did.
Zh: 那是历史上一段有趣的时光。他们发明了晶体管，还有摇滚乐，但却对朊病毒一无所知。完全没人知道。

En: A poor remedy for prion disease, she says. "That was the attitude of most of the more moderate indotribes, particularly those who supported the king..."
Zh: “这是治疗朊病毒的一种糟糕疗法，”她回答到。“大部分更温和的寡头就是这种态度，特别是那些国王的支持者……”

En: A city state divided into free market zones. Under the *everlasting* interregnum of the Coalition of Nations. And you, of course -- the Citizens Militia.
Zh: “一个城邦国家分裂成了自由市场区域。*永远*处于联盟国的过渡期。当然了，还有你们——公民武装。”

En: The clatter of typewriter keys fills the main hall of a re-appropriated Silk Mill -- Precinct 41. Chad Tillbrook presses ENTER. Outside: Officer *Elfboy* Williams slams the door of an armoured motor carriage...
Zh: 打字机按键的咔哒声充斥着丝绸制造厂的大厅——那里是41分局。查德·提尔布鲁克按下了输入键。外面：*精灵小子*威廉姆斯警官嘭的一声关上了装甲車的大门……

En: No way.
Zh: “不是吧。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building.
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。

En: The wind stops, there is silence on the dark water of the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，马丁内斯水湾黑暗的水面上，一片寂静……

En: Probably.
Zh: “也许吧。”

En: No -- almost certainly. The Commune would have forced you. Such was the fate of the undecided.
Zh: “不——几乎可以肯定。公社会强迫你的。这就是悬而未决的命运。”

En: There is curiosity there that goes unnoticed. The lieutenant takes a step back, absent-mindedly.
Zh: 那里有一些潜伏着的好奇心。警督心不在焉地往后退了一步。

En: She turns to him. "And what is *your* official position, lieutenant?"
Zh: 她转向他。“那*你的*官方立场是什么呢，警督？”

En: ALWAYS.
Zh: 总是如此。

En: Forty-three years. Hard to fathom, I know.
Zh: “43年。很难捉摸透，我明白的。”

En: It's been like this for how long, exactly?
Zh: “这种情况，到底已经持续多久了？”

En: Time flies.
Zh: “时光如梭啊。”

En: Wait, you're saying it's been like this for 43 years?
Zh: “等等，你是说这种情况已经持续43年了？”

En: When was this kerfuffle?
Zh: “动乱是什么时候爆发的？”

En: When was this kerfuffle?
Zh: “动乱是什么时候爆发的？”

En: The cold runs down your spine as you say the words, like a small electric charge that seeks grounding.
Zh: 当字句从你口中吐出，一股寒意沿着你的脊椎一路向下，就像一股寻求接地的小电流。

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The charge dissipates into the dark water. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。控诉消散在黑水中。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: They picked the winning side. That's why they're here and others are not.
Zh: “他们选择了胜利的一边。所以他们现在还在这里，而其他人没有。”

En: With some hygiene, modest social care and perhaps a little research program.
Zh: “用点卫生学，再来点适当的社会关怀，也许再加点研究项目。”

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The charge dissipates into the dark water. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。控诉消散在黑水中。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: The Turn-of-the-Century Revolution? She smiles, mischievously. "Don't answer it -- it's a trick question."
Zh: “世纪之交大革命？”她淘气地笑了。“别回答——这个偏题了。”

En: Always, She nods. "That's the *can't-do-attitude* that truly defines Late Modernity. You may prove a Modern Man yet."
Zh: “一直如此，”她点点头。“这种*什么都做不了的态度*就是晚期现代性的最佳定义。也许你确实是个现代人。”

En: They chose wisely.
Zh: “他们的选择很明智。”

En: They got lucky.
Zh: “他们很走运。”

En: A dog barks, a gunshot echoes off the walls of some distant building. A woman's voice...
Zh: 一只狗在吠叫，一声枪响在远处建筑的墙壁上回荡。还有一个女人的声音……

En: Perhaps it wouldn't have turned out that way had *I* been in charge. I might've bet on the king and led the Pines to doom.
Zh: “如果交给*我*来负责的话，也许就不会变成这样。我可能会把赌注押在国王身上，让整个野松公司走向毁灭。”

En: They are what they are -- who knows, an afterbloom may yet come...
Zh: “他们就是他们——谁知道呢，也许还有一股余波没有到来……”

En: The Revolution began in '02, on the isola of Graad, though by the end nearly the whole world had gotten involved.
Zh: “大革命是02年在格拉德洲爆发的，不过最后几乎整个世界都被卷入其中了。”

En: Who started it?
Zh: “谁发起的？”

En: You and everyone else. But that's got nothing to do with *preventing* the Revolution. Ah, well... What was was, I guess.
Zh: “你，还有其他所有人都会这么做。但是这跟*阻止*大革命没有任何关系。嗯……我想，过去的都已经过去了。”

En: You would have sided with the king?
Zh: “你会选择站在国王那一边？”

En: At any cost -- until humanity is free and the age of Capital is past.
Zh: “不惜一切代价——直到全人类解放，直到资本的时代结束。”

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The cold dissipates into your parietal lobe like a dissolved bullet. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。寒冷像一颗子弹慢慢在你的顶叶溶解。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: I asked you -- who are you in all this?
Zh: “我问你——你在其中又扮演了什么样的角色？”

En: I would have positioned myself very precisely. I would have made it out -- with the mineral rights.
Zh: “我会非常精准地站好自己的位置。我会成功的——和矿产权一起。”

En: I would have made off with the mineral rights.
Zh: “我会带着矿产权一起逃跑。”

En: I would have sided with the *cannons*. If you'd seen the calibres of the things, you might've, too...  She thinks. "Perhaps it's better I was born when I was..."
Zh: “我会选择跟*大炮*站在一边。如果你见到那玩意的口径，你可能也会……”她想了想。“也许我最好出生在……”

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The cold dissipates into your parietal lobe like a dissolved bullet. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。寒冷像一颗子弹慢慢在你的顶叶溶解。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: What would you have done differently?
Zh: “如果是你，会有什么不同的做法吗？”

En: Good question. She cranes her neck: "What would *you* have done differently?"
Zh: “这是个好问题。”她伸长了脖子：“那*你*又会怎么做呢？”

En: No -- her first.
Zh: 不——她先说。

En: What happened in the rest of the world? (Proceed.)
Zh: “世界的其它地方呢？”（继续。）

En: Where did it spread from there?
Zh: “是从哪里传播出来的？”

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The cold dissipates into your parietal lobe like a dissolved bullet. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。寒冷像一颗子弹慢慢在你的顶叶溶解。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: And I asked *you*, past-less detective of the Citizens Militia. What insight has acute encephalopathy given to you?
Zh: “我问的是*你*，没有过去的公民武装警探先生。急性脑损伤给你带来了什么领悟呢？”

En: And I asked *you*, past-less detective of the Citizens Militia. What insight has acute encephalopathy given to you?
Zh: “我问的是*你*，没有过去的公民武装警探。急性脑损伤给你带来了什么领悟呢？”

En: The wind stops and for a moment there's silence. The cold dissipates into your parietal lobe like a dissolved bullet. All is quiet on the Martinaise inlet...
Zh: 风停了下来，陷入了片刻的沉默。寒冷像一颗子弹慢慢在你的顶叶溶解。马丁内斯水湾一片寂静……

En: Until this insanity is crushed and Revachol reigns -- a suzerain forever.
Zh: “直到摧毁这一切疯狂，直到瑞瓦肖夺回主权——成为一个永久的宗主国。”

En: And I asked *you*, philosopher-detective of the Citizens Militia.
Zh: “我问的是*你*，公民武装的哲学警探先生。”

En: She narrows her eyes -- turning her gaze to the future out of professional habit...
Zh: 她眯起双眼——出于职业习惯，她将目光转向了未来……

En: What came next?
Zh: “接下来呢？”

En: Coupris, Resplendent... both gone now. Those that survived mended their ways. They're all *ultras* now.
Zh: “库普瑞斯，还有光辉……现在已经全都消逝。那些幸存下来的人们修正了他们的方向。现在他们都是*极端分子*了。”

En: Ten of the fourteen indotribes got it wrong: Feld, Coupris, Tricentennial... So I suppose I would have been in good company.
Zh: “14个寡头中有10个都错了：菲尔德、库普瑞斯、三生……所以我猜我这是优势合作吧。”

En: In the end they liquidated the Sol Aurum. Melted the palace cube down to gold and platinum and sold it for bullets. Strange times, she says almost wistfully.
Zh: “最后，他们查封了索尔·奥利姆。把宫殿熔成了金子和铂金，然后卖掉换成子弹。真是个奇怪的时代，”她若有所思的说到。

En: From Revachol and Graad? Not far. The world managed to cauterize itself. Mazov's government was overthrown in '08, and the Coalition crushed the Revachol commune two years later. It was *The End*.
Zh: “从瑞瓦肖和格拉德？不远。世界自己腐蚀了一切。马佐夫政府在08年被推翻，两年后联盟摧毁了瑞瓦肖公社。这就是*结局*。”

En: Why, you and I, officer-- She spreads her arms, raincoat flapping in the wind. "Our lives in the Zone of Control."
Zh: “没什么，你和我，警官——”她伸开双臂，雨衣在风雨中摇摆。“我们生活在这个控制区。”

En: I would have killed more -- 400 million -- if that's what it took.
Zh: “我会杀掉更多人——4个亿——如果必须这样的话。”

En: I see -- a tenth of humanity. She's unfazed. "The key of History is in the lock -- keep turning, at any cost?"
Zh: “我知道了——十分之一的人类。”她并没有感到苦恼。“历史的钥匙就插在锁眼里——不惜一切代价，也要继续转动，是吧？”

En: Something tells you her life and yours are not that similar.
Zh: 有些东西告诉你，你和她的生活不太一样。

En: It wasn't a *who*, but a *what*. A pandemic of tzaraath, a particularly virulent prion disease, which the authorities in Graad proved unable to contain. Then Mazov came along and overthrew the government.
Zh: “不是说*谁*，而是*什么*。一场霉斑瘟疫，一种非常致命的朊病毒，格拉德当局无法控制的病毒。然后马佐夫出现，推翻了政府。”

En: Understandable, she nods.
Zh: “可以理解，”她点点头。

En: Anyway, enough sentimentality. Is there anything else you want to know?
Zh: “总之，多愁善感的事已经说的差不多了。你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: When they failed to step up, Mazov and his party stepped in. In this *particular* case, maybe a more robust state response might have been appropriate...
Zh: “他们没有挺身而出的时候，马佐夫和他的党派插了进来。在这种*特定*的情况下，更强势一点的回应可能更合时宜……”
